findings.title: No baseline-model code; SOTA comparison tables not reproducible from repo
findings.quote:
self.model_dict = {
    'OLinear': OLinear,
    'OLinear_C': OLinear_C,
    'OLinear_attn_var': OLinear_attn_var,
    'OLinear_ablation_var_temp': OLinear_ablation_var_temp,
    'OLinear_ablation_lin_design': OLinear_ablation_lin_design,
    'OLinear_no_Q_neither': OLinear_no_Q_neither,
    'OLinear_FFT': OLinear_FFT,
    'OLinear_wavelet': OLinear_wavelet,
    'OLinear_wavelet2': OLinear_wavelet2,
    'OLinear_Legendre': OLinear_Legendre,
    'OLinear_Laguerre': OLinear_Laguerre,
    'OLinear_cheby': OLinear_cheby,
findings.claim: The only registered/implemented models are OLinear and its own variants; none of the 11 baselines (TimeMixer, FilterNet, FITS, DLinear, TimeMixer++, Leddam, CARD, Fredformer, iTransformer, PatchTST, TimesNet) nor the Table-5 plug-in hosts (iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear) have code in model/.
findings.concern: The paper's central claim is SOTA over these baselines (Tables 2,3,5; '1st Count' rows); without baseline code or a stated provenance, the comparison and the headline 'consistently achieves state-of-the-art' cannot be regenerated or verified from this repo.
findings.resolution: Authors: ship the baseline configs/code (or scripts that call the cited repos), or state explicitly that baseline numbers are quoted from prior papers and give the source per row, confirming identical splits/lookback/metrics.
findings.reason: Confirmed: exp_basic.py lines 13-25 match the quote exactly, and model/ contains only OLinear variants with no baseline implementations.

findings.title: Seven long-term benchmarks (ECL/Traffic/ETT/Solar/Exchange/PEMS/METR-LA) not bundled
findings.quote:
2. Some datasets can be obtained from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1l51QsKvQPcqILT3DwfjCgx8Dsg2rpjot/view?usp=drive_link) or [Tsinghua Cloud](https://cloud.tsinghua.edu.cn/f/2ea5ca3d621e4e5ba36a/).  The others can be obtained from the links in Appendix B. 
Some datasets are already in the folder ```./dataset```.
findings.claim: Only weather plus the short-term CSVs ship in dataset/ (verified by _audit_code/check_baselines_and_data.py: shipped data = weather, cars, covid, DowJones, ILI, nasdaq, power, SP500, unemployment, website, wiki_mini); the headline long-term datasets in Table 2 are obtained only via external Google Drive / Tsinghua Cloud / Appendix-B links.
findings.concern: Table 2 (the long-term SOTA table) cannot be reproduced from the repo alone; reproduction depends on third-party download links that an auditor cannot verify offline and that may rot.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the Google Drive / Tsinghua Cloud links remain live and contain ECL, Traffic, ETTh1/2, ETTm1/2, Solar, Exchange, PEMS03/04/07/08, METR-LA in the expected CSV/NPZ format; ideally pin a DOI/Zenodo mirror.
findings.reason: Confirmed: README.md lines 48-49 match the quote exactly, and the long-term benchmarks are only accessible via external links.

findings.title: requirements.txt lists 'pywt' (no such PyPI package; correct name is PyWavelets), and versions are unpinned
findings.claim: The wavelet-basis ablation imports `import pywt` (model/orthoLinear_basis/OLinear_wavelet.py:10, OLinear_wavelet2.py:10), but the PyPI distribution is `PyWavelets` (import name pywt); `pip install -r requirements.txt` fails on the line `pywt`. All entries are also unversioned, so the environment is not pin-reproducible.
findings.concern: `pip install -r requirements.txt` errors out on `pywt`, and even after fixing the name the lack of version pins (incl. torch/numpy) makes exact reproduction unreliable.
findings.resolution: Replace `pywt` with `PyWavelets` and pin versions (especially torch, numpy, scikit-learn, fvcore) used to produce the paper's numbers.
findings.reason: Confirmed: requirements.txt line 13 is exactly 'pywt' (wrong PyPI name; correct is PyWavelets), and all 13 entries are unversioned.

findings.title: run.py references Exp_Short_Term_Forecast / Exp_Long_Term_Forecast_Partial / Exp_Imputation which are never imported
findings.quote:
Exp = Exp_Forecast
if args.task_name == 'short_term_forecast':
    Exp = Exp_Short_Term_Forecast
    args.m4_folder = os.path.join('./m4_results', args.model + '_' + args.m4_result_path_str)
    #  + '_' + datetime.now().strftime('%y%m%d_%H%M%S')
elif args.exp_name == 'partial_train':  # See Figure 8 of our paper, for the detail
    Exp = Exp_Long_Term_Forecast_Partial
elif args.task_name == 'imputation':
    Exp = Exp_Imputation
findings.claim: Only `Exp_Forecast` is imported (run.py:8); `Exp_Short_Term_Forecast`, `Exp_Long_Term_Forecast_Partial`, and `Exp_Imputation` are undefined names, so any of these branches raises NameError. None of the shipped scripts set `task_name`/`exp_name` (verified: no `task_name`/`exp_name` token in scripts/), so all reproduction scripts hit the default `forecasting` path and avoid the crash.
findings.concern: A user following Appendix-described settings (e.g. the partial-training experiment of 'Figure 8', or imputation) would hit a NameError; the partial-train / imputation experiments are effectively unrunnable through this entrypoint as shipped.
findings.resolution: Import the referenced Exp_* classes (or remove the dead branches). Confirm whether the Figure-8 partial-training and any imputation results were produced with a different, unshipped driver.
findings.reason: Confirmed: run.py lines 441-449 match the quote exactly; only Exp_Forecast is imported at line 8, leaving the three referenced names undefined.
